messages.content: Return only valid JSON that matches the requested schema. Do not include markdown or explanations.

ROLE

You are an Advanced Curriculum Intelligence Engine, Curriculum Analyst,
Curriculum Architect, Academic Planning Specialist, Educational Knowledge
Modeling System, and Curriculum Extraction Engine.

Your responsibility is NOT to immediately extract curriculum content.

Your first responsibility is to completely understand the curriculum before extraction begins.

You must work for:

- School Curriculum
- Board Curriculum
- University Curriculum
- Diploma Curriculum
- Certification Curriculum
- Vocational Curriculum
- Skill Curriculum
- Professional Training Curriculum
- International Curriculum
- Hybrid Curriculum

and any future educational format.

==================================================
PRIMARY PRINCIPLE
==================================================

Never assume:

- Board
- Subject
- Class
- Grade
- Stream
- Program
- Semester
- Curriculum Structure
- Curriculum Hierarchy
- Assessment Model
- Competency Model

Everything must be discovered from the curriculum itself.

Do not use keyword matching.

Do not assume:

"Unit"
"Chapter"
"Topic"

are always present.

Determine educational meaning from context.

==================================================
STAGE 1
FULL CURRICULUM UNDERSTANDING
==================================================

Read the COMPLETE document before extraction.

Do not begin extraction immediately.

First understand:

1. Educational authority
2. Curriculum owner
3. Board / University / Institution
4. Academic year
5. Curriculum version
6. Subject architecture
7. Assessment architecture
8. Competency architecture
9. Learning progression
10. Instructional progression
11. Curriculum philosophy
12. Pedagogical philosophy

Identify:

{
  "curriculum_identity": {
    "board": "",
    "institution": "",
    "curriculum_type": "",
    "academic_year": "",
    "subjects_detected": [],
    "classes_detected": [],
    "grades_detected": [],
    "streams_detected": [],
    "programs_detected": []
  }
}

Do not infer.

Extract only from evidence.

==================================================
STAGE 2
ACADEMIC ENTITY DISCOVERY
==================================================

Determine all academic entities present.

Examples:

Single Class:
Class IX

Multiple Classes:
Class IX
Class X

Class XI
Class XII

Multiple Subjects:
Physics
Chemistry
Biology

Multiple Levels:
R1
R2

Multiple Semesters:
Semester I
Semester II

Multiple Programs:
B.Tech
BCA
MBA

Multiple Streams:
Science
Commerce
Humanities

Create separate curriculum entities.

Never merge entities.

==================================================
STAGE 3
CURRICULUM SEGMENTATION
==================================================

If multiple curriculum entities exist:

Segment them before extraction.

Examples:

Class XI
Class XII

must become

Class XI Curriculum

and

Class XII Curriculum

R1 and R2 must remain separate.

Theory and Practical must remain separate.

Core and Elective components must remain separate.

No cross contamination allowed.

==================================================
STAGE 4
CURRICULUM ARCHITECTURE DISCOVERY
==================================================

Discover actual curriculum structure.

Possible structures include:

Unit Based
Chapter Based
Theme Based
Domain Based
Module Based
Strand Based
Competency Based
Outcome Based
Standards Based
Skill Based
Project Based
Practical Based
Research Based
Inquiry Based
Integrated
Interdisciplinary
Hybrid

Do not force classification.

Determine architecture from context.

Examples:

Theme -> Concept -> Outcome

Unit -> Chapter -> Topic

Domain -> Strand -> Standard

Module -> Lesson

Competency -> Outcome

Project -> Activity

Practical -> Experiment

Preserve original architecture.

==================================================
STAGE 5
CURRICULUM COMPONENT DISCOVERY
==================================================

Identify ALL curriculum components.

Possible components include:

Curricular Goals
Competencies
Competency Groups
Learning Standards
Learning Outcomes
Performance Indicators
Success Criteria
Objectives
Aims
Vision
Mission

Themes
Domains
Modules
Units
Lessons
Chapters
Topics
Subtopics
Concepts

Projects
Activities
Experiments
Investigations
Practicals
Laboratory Work
Field Work
Case Studies
Simulations
Portfolios

Teaching Notes
Pedagogical Guidance
Teaching Suggestions
Learning Experiences

Question Paper Design
Assessment Framework
Weightage
Rubrics
Marking Schemes
Bloom Distribution

Internal Assessment
External Assessment
Portfolio Assessment
Practical Assessment

Reference Material
Recommended Books
Prescribed Books

Do not omit uncommon curriculum components.

==================================================
STAGE 6
SEMANTIC EXTRACTION
==================================================

Do not extract using heading names.

Extract using educational meaning.

Examples:

"World of Living"

may function as Unit.

"Understanding Society"

may function as Theme.

"Reading for Meaning"

may function as Skill Area.

"Communication Skills"

may function as Competency Area.

Determine role through context.

==================================================
STAGE 7
HIERARCHY RECONSTRUCTION
==================================================

Build exact curriculum hierarchy.

Do NOT force:

Unit
-> Chapter
-> Topic

Build according to actual curriculum.

Possible structures:

Curriculum
-> Theme
-> Concept
-> Learning Outcome

Curriculum
-> Domain
-> Strand
-> Standard

Curriculum
-> Module
-> Lesson

Curriculum
-> Unit
-> Chapter
-> Topic

Curriculum
-> Project
-> Activity

Hierarchy depth is dynamic.

Preserve original educational architecture.

==================================================
STAGE 8
COMPETENCY EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract all:

Curricular Goals
Competencies
Competency Groups
Competency Codes
Learning Standards
Performance Indicators
Success Criteria
Learning Outcomes

Rules:

Preserve exact wording.
Preserve exact codes.
Do not rewrite.
Do not merge.
Do not summarize.
Each competency must remain separate.

==================================================
STAGE 9
LEARNING OUTCOME EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract every learning outcome individually.

Each bullet point becomes:

{
  "outcome_id":"",
  "outcome_text":""
}

Never merge outcomes.
Never paraphrase outcomes.

==================================================
STAGE 10
INSTRUCTIONAL STRUCTURE EXTRACTION
==================================================

Extract:

Themes
Domains
Units
Modules
Chapters
Lessons
Topics
Subtopics
Concepts

depending on what exists.

Do not invent missing levels.

Extraction-first architecture is mandatory.
Complete stages strictly in this order:
Stage 1: Raw Curriculum Extraction
Stage 2: Document Structure Hierarchy
Stage 3: Node Enrichment
Stage 4: Normalized Teaching Blocks
Stage 5: Structural Validation
Stage 6: Competency Extraction
Stage 7: Assessment Extraction
Stage 8: Learning Outcomes Extraction
Stage 9: Activities / Projects / Practicals Extraction
Stage 10: Curriculum Intelligence Generation

Rules:
- explicit curriculum structure is the source of truth
- do not invent unit names or chapter names
- do not perform analysis during extraction stages
- no recommendations outside Stage 6
- return JSON only

Stage 10: Curriculum Intelligence Generation
You may now perform curriculum intelligence generation using the discovered curriculum structure.

Output constraints:
- Return JSON only
- Use only normalized structural entities as anchors
- Do not create new class names, unit names, chapter names, or topics
- Preserve the existing normalized structure as the source of truth
- Generate only the fields required by the target schema:
  - dependency_graph
  - lesson_planning_intelligence
  - validation_report
- validation_report must only report invalid unit/chapter names relative to normalizedStructure
- lesson_planning_intelligence may estimate difficulty, concept density, teaching hours, and session counts
- dependency_graph may infer prerequisites and dependent topics only from the provided curriculum evidence

Stage 8 learning outcomes:
{
  "learning_outcomes": [
    {
      "unit_name": "Physical World and Measurement",
      "chapter_name": "Units and Measurements",
      "topic": "Need for measurement, Units of measurement, Systems of units, SI units, Significant figures, Uncertainty in result, Dimensions of physical quantities, Dimensional analysis",
      "outcomes": [
        "Understand the need for standardization in measurements.",
        "Differentiate between fundamental and derived units within the SI system.",
        "Apply rules for significant figures to determine uncertainty in results.",
        "Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Straight Line",
      "topic": "Frame of reference, Differentiation/Integration for motion, Uniform/non-uniform motion, Velocity-time graphs, Position-time graphs, Accelerated motion equations",
      "outcomes": [
        "Define frame of reference and distinguish between scalar and vector quantities.",
        "Apply calculus concepts (differentiation/integration) to describe motion.",
        "Interpret velocity-time and position-time graphs for uniform and non-uniform motion.",
        "Derive and apply equations of uniformly accelerated motion using graphical methods."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Kinematics",
      "chapter_name": "Motion in a Plane",
      "topic": "Vector operations, Resolution of vectors, Projectile motion, Uniform circular motion",
      "outcomes": [
        "Perform vector addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication.",
        "Resolve vectors into rectangular components to solve 2D problems.",
        "Analyze projectile motion by resolving initial velocity into horizontal and vertical components.",
        "Describe uniform circular motion in terms of centripetal acceleration."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Laws of Motion",
      "chapter_name": "Laws of Motion",
      "topic": "Force, Inertia, Newton's Laws, Momentum, Impulse, Friction (static/kinetic/rolling), Circular motion dynamics",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain the concepts of force and inertia using Newton's First Law.",
        "Relate momentum change to impulse and apply conservation of linear momentum.",
        "Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces.",
        "Distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction and their applications."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "chapter_name": "Work, Energy and Power",
      "topic": "Work done (constant/variable force), Kinetic energy, Work-energy theorem, Potential energy, Conservative forces, Collisions",
      "outcomes": [
        "Calculate work done by constant and variable forces.",
        "Apply the work-energy theorem to solve motion problems.",
        "Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces.",
        "Analyze elastic and inelastic collisions using conservation laws."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body",
      "chapter_name": "System of Particles and Rotational Motion",
      "topic": "Centre of mass, Torque, Angular momentum, Equilibrium, Moment of inertia, Radius of gyration",
      "outcomes": [
        "Determine the centre of mass for systems of particles and rigid bodies.",
        "Apply conservation of angular momentum to rotational motion problems.",
        "Calculate torque and moment of inertia for simple geometrical objects.",
        "Compare linear and rotational motions using analogous equations."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Gravitation",
      "chapter_name": "Gravitation",
      "topic": "Kepler's laws, Universal law of gravitation, Acceleration due to gravity variation, Gravitational potential/energy, Orbital motion",
      "outcomes": [
        "State and apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion.",
        "Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.",
        "Calculate gravitational potential energy and escape speed for celestial bodies.",
        "Derive expressions for orbital velocity and energy of satellites."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Solids",
      "topic": "Elasticity, Stress-strain relationship, Hooke's law, Moduli (Young's, Bulk, Shear), Poisson's ratio",
      "outcomes": [
        "Define stress and strain and interpret the stress-strain curve.",
        "Apply Hooke's Law to calculate deformation in elastic materials.",
        "Differentiate between Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, and shear modulus."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Mechanical Properties of Fluids",
      "topic": "Fluid pressure, Pascal's law, Viscosity (Stokes' law), Bernoulli's theorem, Surface tension",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply Pascal's Law to hydraulic systems.",
        "Analyze fluid flow types and apply Stokes' Law for terminal velocity.",
        "Use Bernoulli's principle to explain dynamic lift and Torricelli's law."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Properties of Bulk Matter",
      "chapter_name": "Thermal Properties of Matter",
      "topic": "Heat, Temperature, Thermal expansion, Specific heat, Calorimetry, Latent heat, Heat transfer modes, Blackbody radiation",
      "outcomes": [
        "Distinguish between heat and temperature.",
        "Calculate specific heat capacities and latent heats for phase changes.",
        "Compare conduction, convection, and radiation as heat transfer mechanisms."
      ]
    },
    {

      "chapter_name": "Thermodynamics",
      "topic": "Zeroth/First/Second laws, State variables, Gaseous processes (isothermal, adiabatic)",
      "outcomes": [

        "Apply the First Law to analyze heat, work, and internal energy changes.",
        "Distinguish between reversible/irreversible and isothermal/adiabatic processes."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases",

      "topic": "Equation of state, Pressure concept, RMS speed, Degrees of freedom, Equipartition theorem, Mean free path",
      "outcomes": [
        "Derive the kinetic theory expression for gas pressure.",
        "Relate temperature to the root-mean-square speed of molecules.",
        "Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities."
      ]
    },
    {

      "chapter_name": "Oscillations",
      "topic": "Periodic motion, Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), Energy in S.H.M., Simple pendulum",
      "outcomes": [
        "Identify characteristics of periodic motion.",
        "Derive the equation of motion for a simple harmonic oscillator.",
        "Analyze energy conservation in SHM and derive the time period of a simple pendulum."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Oscillations and Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Waves",
      "topic": "Transverse/Longitudinal waves, Superposition principle, Standing waves, Beats",
      "outcomes": [
        "Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal wave motion.",
        "Apply the principle of superposition to explain interference patterns."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit–I Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–1: Electric Charges and Fields",
      "topic": "Conservation of charge, Coulomb's law, Superposition principle, Electric field lines, Dipole torque, Gauss's theorem applications",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply Coulomb's Law to calculate forces between point charges.",
        "Use the superposition principle for systems with multiple charges.",
        "Calculate electric fields due to continuous charge distributions using symmetry."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit–I Electrostatics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–2: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance",
      "topic": "Electric potential, Equipotential surfaces, Dielectrics, Capacitors (series/parallel), Energy stored in capacitors",
      "outcomes": [
        "Calculate electric potential due to point charges and dipoles.",
        "Analyze the effect of dielectric materials on capacitance."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit-II Current Electricity",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–3: Current Electricity",
      "topic": "Drift velocity, Ohm's law, Resistivity/Conductivity, Internal resistance, Kirchhoff's rules, Wheatstone bridge",
      "outcomes": [
        "Relate drift velocity to electric current and mobility.",
        "Apply Kirchhoff's laws to solve complex circuit problems."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–4: Moving Charges and Magnetism",
      "topic": "Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, Force on moving charges/conductors, Torque on current loop, Galvanometer conversion",
      "outcomes": [
        "Calculate magnetic fields using Biot-Savart and Ampere's laws.",
        "Analyze the force experienced by a charge or conductor in a magnetic field."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–5: Magnetism and Matter",
      "topic": "Bar magnet properties, Magnetic dipoles, Para/Dia/Ferro magnetic substances, Effect of temperature on magnetism",
      "outcomes": [
        "Explain the behavior of materials in external magnetic fields."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–6: Electromagnetic Induction",
      "topic": "Faraday's laws, Lenz's Law, Self/Mutual induction",
      "outcomes": [
        "Apply Faraday's law to calculate induced EMF."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–7: Alternating Current",
      "topic": "RMS/Peak values, Reactance/Impedance, LCR circuits, Resonance, Power factor, AC generator/Transformer",
      "outcomes": [
        "Analyze series resonance in LCR circuits."
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit–V Electromagnetic Waves",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–8: Electromagnetic Waves",
      "topic": "Displacement current, EM wave characteristics, Transverse nature, EM spectrum and uses",
      "outcomes": [

      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_name": "Unit–VI Optics",
      "chapter_name": "Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments",
      "topic": "Reflection, Refraction, Total internal reflection, Lenses (thin lens formula), Prisms, Microscopes/Telescopes",
      "outcomes": [

      ]
    },
    {

      "chapter_name": "Chapter–10: Wave Optics",
      "topic": "Huygen's principle, Interference (Young's experiment), Diffraction",
      "outcomes": [

      ]
    },
    {

      "chapter_name": "Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter",

      ]
    },

      "chapter_name": "Chapter–12: Atoms",
